Product Id: Monthly Electricity Supply and Disposition Survey
For monthly data prior to January 2008, please see table 25-10-0001-01 (terminated). For current monthly data on electric power generation, see also table 25-10-0015-01.
Total generation includes nuclear, hydro, tidal, wind, solar, wave, geothermal, other non-combustible methods and combustible fuel generation.
Nationally, this is the sum of purchased receipts and other receipts from the United States. Provincially, this is the sum of electricity received from other provinces and from the United States.
The sum of receipts of electricity from other provinces.
Electric energy received from the United States.
Nationally, this is the sum of deliveries of electricity to the United States. Provincially, this is the sum of electricity delivered to other provinces and to the United States.
The sum of deliveries (firm and non-firm) of electricity to other provinces.
Electric energy (firm, non-firm and other) transferred to the United States.

Electricity intended to be available at all times during the period of the agreement of its sale, even under adverse conditions, but subject to unforeseen interruptions. This is the sum of firm deliveries to other provinces and firm deliveries to the United States.

Electricity intended to be available for delivery to other provinces at all times during the period of the agreement of its sale, even under adverse conditions, but subject to force majeure interruptions.

Electricity intended to be available for delivery at all times to the United States during the period of the agreement of its sale, even under adverse conditions, but subject to force majeure interruptions.

Transactions for short periods and subject to curtailment or cessation of delivery by the supplier or purchaser in accordance with prior agreements or under specific conditions. This is the sum of non-firm deliveries to other provinces and non-firm deliveries to the United States.
Transactions for short periods and subject to curtailment or cessation of delivery to other provinces by the supplier or purchaser in accordance with prior agreements or under specific conditions.
Transactions for short periods and subject to curtailment or cessation of delivery to the United States by the supplier or purchaser in accordance with prior agreements or under specific conditions.
Electricity given in compensation to the United States.
Total electricity available for use (total generation plus total receipts less total deliveries).

The target population for the Monthly Electricity Supply and Disposition Survey has been expanded to improve coverage of renewable energy in Canada, particularly wind and solar power. As a result of this change, users will have to use caution when comparing data for historical purposes. For wind power, significant changes occurred in British Columbia, Alberta, Ontario, Quebec and Prince Edward Island. For solar power, significant changes occurred in Ontario.
